name: Promenade of Les Rotes
name_original: Cala Les Rotes — ida y vuelta desde el puerto de Dénia
description: Hike the moderate 5.7-mile Cala Les Rotes round trip from Dénia port, enjoying coastal views and diverse terrain in Montgó Natural Park.
region_intro: Hiking around Dénia offers diverse natural landscapes, centered around the imposing Montgó Natural Park. This region features limestone massifs, dense pine forests, and steep cliffs that provide panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea. The coastline is characterized by rocky coves, marine reserves, and extensive sandy beaches, with historical agricultural areas including orange and almond groves also present. Best hiking trails around Dénia The most popular hiking route is Cova Tallada and Torre del Gerro Loop, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines coastal paths with views of a historic watchtower and a unique sea cave. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Montgó — circular from Jávea, a difficult 9.4 miles (15.2 km) path. This challenging route offers significant elevation gain and expansive views from the Montgó summit. Local hikers also love the Cima del Montgó — circular por las rutas VERDE, NARANJA y AZUL, an 11.1 miles (17.9 km) trail leading through Montgó Natural Park, often completed in about 6 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Dénia is defined by majestic mountains, pristine coastlines, and wooded areas. The network offers options for various ability levels, from gentle walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Dénia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1700 reviews. More than 11000 hikers have used komoot to explore Dénia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Dénia region?
A: Dénia offers an extensive network of trails, with over 250 hiking routes documented on komoot. These range from easy coastal strolls to challenging mountain ascents, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on Dénia's hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Dénia is incredibly diverse. You'll find limestone massifs, dense pine forests, and steep cliffs within the Montgó Natural Park, offering panoramic sea views. The coastline features rocky coves and marine reserves, while inland areas include historical agricultural landscapes with orange and almond groves.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia has over 100 easy routes suitable for families and casual walkers. A great option is the Dénia Greenway (Vía Verde), an old railway line that is flat and passes through beautiful orange groves with views of Montgó. The 'Cliffs Route' is also suitable for beginners, offering lovely Mediterranean views.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Dénia?
A: For experienced hikers, the ascent to the Montgó summit offers significant elevation gain and unparalleled panoramic views. Consider routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870232585/el-montgo-circular-from-javea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Montgó — circular from Jávea&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222769987/cima-del-montgo-circular-por-las-rutas-verde-naranja-y-azul" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cima del Montgó — circular por las rutas VERDE, NARANJA y AZUL&lt;/a&gt; for a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Dénia?
A: Yes, Dénia features several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870318867/cova-tallada-and-torre-del-gerro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada and Torre del Gerro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines coastal paths with views of a historic watchtower and a unique sea cave. The Montgó Circular Path also offers spectacular views of the coast and inland.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Dénia?
A: Dénia's trails lead to many fascinating sights. You can explore the unique half-natural, half-artificial sea cave, Cova Tallada, or visit the 16th-century watchtower, Torre del Gerro, for amazing views. The Cova de l'Aigua, located on Montgó, features a small pond and a Roman inscription. For more natural beauty, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5407844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marjal de Pego-Oliva Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4866511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural area of the "Font Salà"&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about Dénia's trails?
A: The hiking routes in Dénia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1700 reviews. Hikers often praise the majestic mountain views, the pristine coastlines, and the variety of well-maintained trails that cater to all levels.

Q: Are there any coastal walks with sea views?
A: Absolutely. The Les Rotes Coastal Path is a scenic area characterized by pristine rocky coves and crystal-clear waters, offering uninterrupted sea views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1223173874/promenade-of-les-rotes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Promenade of Les Rotes&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that allows you to enjoy this beautiful coastline.

Q: Can I find any historical sites or castles near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Dénia has a rich history. While hiking, you might encounter historical watchtowers like the Torre del Gerro. You can also find several castles in the wider region, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moraira Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/778482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet d'Orba Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the area's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Dénia?
A: Dénia's Mediterranean climate makes hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer treks. Even winter can be suitable, with mild weather. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this season.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Balearic Islands?
A: Yes, from the higher elevations of Montgó Natural Park, particularly from the summit trails, you can enjoy breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea. On clear days, it's even possible to spot the Balearic Islands, such as Ibiza, in the distance.
tips: On this side of Denia, there are some small beaches, but they're less accessible and have a lot of aquatic plants. (On the other side of Denia, there are large, clean beaches.)
Along the way between Dénia and Cala de Las Rotas you will have wonderful views of the sea. There are also several restaurants.
The Mirador de la Cala d'Aigua Dolç is a beautiful vantage point. This cove is known for its crystal-clear waters and natural surroundings, making it a popular spot for naturism and snorkeling.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. During summer, it's best to start early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat. The coastal path provides refreshing sea breezes.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, while rated moderate, the trail is generally considered accessible and suitable for most skill levels, including beginners and families. The initial promenade sections are wide and well-maintained. Some parts towards Cala Les Rotes become a natural stone pedestrian path, but the minimal elevation gain makes it manageable for all ages.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse terrain. You'll start on a paved promenade, sidewalk, and asphalt, transitioning to natural stone pedestrian paths along the coast. Expect rocky coves and pebble beaches, particularly in the Punta Negra area. It's a varied walk with both smooth and uneven surfaces.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Cala Les Rotes trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the trail. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in more crowded areas and within the Cabo de San Antonio Marine Reserve to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the trail or Montgó Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits to hike this trail, and there are no entrance fees for accessing the route or the Montgó Natural Park area through which it passes. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park near the trailhead at the Port of Dénia?
faqs.answer: Parking is available around the Port of Dénia, though it can be busy, especially during peak season. There are public parking areas in and around the port, some paid and some free. Arriving early is recommended to secure a spot, particularly if you plan to hike on weekends or holidays.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning coastal and mountain views. You'll enjoy wonderful views of the sea, especially between Dénia and Cala de Les Rotas. Highlights include the Las Rotas Beach, the View of Dénia and the Sea, and Views from the Cape. You'll also pass through the Cabo de San Antonio Marine Reserve, known for its clear waters, and see picturesque coves like El Trampolí and Les Arenetes. In the distance, you can spot the historic Torre del Gerro watchtower.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially along the initial promenade section, there are several traditional restaurants with sea-view terraces where you can stop for a meal or a drink. As you get closer to Cala Les Rotes, options become fewer, so it's wise to carry water and snacks.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers 100% of the official OpenStreetMap trail "El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana". It also utilizes significant sections of "Passeig de la Marineta Casiana" and smaller parts of "Carrer Fènix", "Carrer Hidra", "Carrer de Jupiter", and "Camí d'Urios".

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming or snorkeling along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The trail passes through the Cabo de San Antonio Marine Reserve, which is renowned for its clean, transparent waters and vibrant seabeds. Many of the coves, such as El Trampolí and Les Arenetes, offer excellent spots for swimming and snorkeling, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, comfortable walking shoes or light hiking boots are recommended. Bring water, especially on warmer days, and sun protection like a hat and sunscreen. A swimsuit and towel could be useful if you plan to take a dip in the coves. Layers are always a good idea for changing coastal weather.

waypoints.name: View of Dénia and the Sea
